en: A PSALM. A SONG FOR THE SABBATH. It is good to give thanks to the LORD, to sing praises to your name, O Most High;
zh: 稱謝耶和華， 歌頌你至高者的名；

en: to declare your steadfast love in the morning, and your faithfulness by night,
zh: 早晨傳揚你的慈愛， 夜間傳講你的信實；

en: to the music of the lute and the harp, to the melody of the lyre.
zh: 彈奏十弦的樂器和瑟， 又用琴彈出美妙的聲音， 這是多麼美的事 。

en: For you, O LORD, have made me glad by your work; at the works of your hands I sing for joy.
zh: 耶和華啊！你藉著你的作為使我歡喜， 我要因你手的工作歡呼。

en: How great are your works, O LORD! Your thoughts are very deep!
zh: 耶和華啊！你的工作多麼偉大， 你的心思極為深奧。

en: The stupid man cannot know; the fool cannot understand this:
zh: 無理性的人不曉得， 愚昧的人不明白這事：

en: that though the wicked sprout like grass and all evildoers flourish, they are doomed to destruction forever;
zh: 惡人雖好像草一樣繁茂， 所有作孽的人雖然興旺， 他們都要永遠滅亡。

en: but you, O LORD, are on high forever.
zh: 但你耶和華永遠是至高的。

en: For behold, your enemies, O LORD, for behold, your enemies shall perish; all evildoers shall be scattered.
zh: 耶和華啊！你的仇敵必滅亡， 你的仇敵必定滅亡； 所有作孽的都必被分散。

en: But you have exalted my horn like that of the wild ox; you have poured over me fresh oil.
zh: 你卻高舉了我的角，像野牛的角； 你用新油膏抹我。

en: My eyes have seen the downfall of my enemies; my ears have heard the doom of my evil assailants.
zh: 我親眼看見那些窺伺我的人遭報， 我親耳聽見那些起來攻擊我的惡人受罰。

en: The righteous flourish like the palm tree and grow like a cedar in Lebanon.
zh: 義人必像棕樹一樣繁茂， 像黎巴嫩的香柏樹一樣高聳。

en: They are planted in the house of the LORD; they flourish in the courts of our God.
zh: 他們栽在耶和華的殿中， 在我們 神的院子裡繁茂生長。

en: They still bear fruit in old age; they are ever full of sap and green,
zh: 他們年老的時候仍要結果子， 經常保持茂盛青翠，

en: to declare that the LORD is upright; he is my rock, and there is no unrighteousness in him.
zh: 好顯明耶和華是正直的； 他是我的磐石，在他裡面沒有不義。
